Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena On Stripe Inc For Production Of Employment Records And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Or Its Attorneys
Off Calendar Oct-21-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SHIVA SHIRAZI DAVOUDIAN, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 310-553-0308) for defendant GoCardless, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JOSHUA MARSH's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena On Stripe Inc For Production Of Employment Records And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Or Its Attorneys.

Defendant stipulates to the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter discussed.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is denied without prejudice. The court rejects defendant's argument that the motion is untimely. Slagle v. Superior Court (1989) 211 Cal.App.3d 1309, 1312 ("Nothing in the procedure set forth [in CCP 1985.3] suggests that a court lacks jurisdiction to consider a motion to quash if it is brought after the date set forth in the subpoena for production."). In addition, the parties appear to agree that plaintiff's counsel notified defendant's counsel of plaintiff's objections to the subpoena before the subpoenaed party produced the records to defendant's counsel. However, plaintiff failed to file a separate statement in support of the motion, which is required under CRC 3.1345(a)(5), nor does it appear that counsel for the parties adequately met and conferred to narrow scope of the subpoena prior to filing the instant motion. No sanctions as to this motion but sanctions may be awarded against either party if the motion is renewed.

Counsel for defendant is instructed to prepare a proposed order in Word format, submit it to opposing counsel for approval as to form, and then submit it to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR No. 13714, Email: apourtabib@gmail.com, Tel: 415-601-7054 (302/JPT)
